LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ZURICH AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS "LEGALLY LIABLE" FOR THE FUNDS IN THE POOLED ACCOUNTS. (SHUMM DECLARATION PARAGRAPH 16, EX 1-2004 AGREEMENT, PP 25, 27; AMFS 3-6, 9-12, 14-15, 18.) TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED THAT DEFENDANT DELIBERATELY IGNORED THE FACTS PRESENTED BY THE INSURED WHICH DEMONSTRATED COVERAGE, CHANNELED ITS INVESTIGATION TO TRY TO SHOW THAT AN EXCLUSION APPLIED, MISREPRESENTED THE COVERAGE TO THE INSURED, AND REFUSED TO CONTACT OTHER PARTIES, INCLUDING WELLS FARGO, TO ASCERTAIN WHETHER THE PLAINTIFF HELD AND WAS LEGALLY LIABLE FOR THE FUNDS. (AMFS 21-28.) (IN ADDITION, THE COURT RULED ON DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE, SUSTAINED 1, 4, 5. COURT STATED NOT ADOPTING ANY LEGAL CONCLUSIONS IN THE DECLARATION/EVIDENCE.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
JURY FEES, TRAN # W2111A03J024, TAG # 029323 DEPOSITED BY PLAINTIFF IRA SERVICES INC.
ORDER DENYING MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENA

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF IRA SERVICES INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENA IS DENIED. THIS MOTION SHOULD BE HEARD BY A NEW YORK COURT. EVEN IF THIS COURT IS THE PROPER FORUM TO HEAR THE MOTION, THE MOTION WOULD BE DENIED DUE TO LACK OF MERIT BASED ON THE DEFENDANT'S REPRESENTATION THAT IT IS NOT SEEKING ANY INFORMATION OF INDIVIDUAL CUSTOMERS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, NOT REPORTED.

GIORGI; NOT REPORTED ---- AFTER HEARING THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FIRST AMENDED REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED. THE INFORMATION SOUGHT BY PLAINTIFF RELATING TO THE LOSS RESERVES IS REASONABLY CALCULATED TO LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE ON THE ISSUE OF WHETHER DEFENDANT CONDUCTED REASONABLE INVESTIGATION, WAS FOLLOWING ITS OWN INTERNAL PROCEDURES FOR PROPER INVESTIGATION AND WAS ACTING IN GOOD OR BAD FAITH IN DENYING PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. THE FACTS PRESENTED HERE ARE SIMILAR TO THOSE IN LIPTON WHICH THE COURT FINDS CONTROLLING. PARTIES NOTIFIED OF THE COURT'S RULING BY TELEPHONE. PLAINTIFF IS DIRECTED TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

MINI MINUTES:

Brian R. Thompson, Esq., from Sedgwick LLP, 415-781-7900, for defendant Zurich Insurance
Company
No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted.
PLAINTIFF IRA SERVICES INC.'s Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena is denied.
This motion should be heard by a New York court. Even if this court is the proper forum to hear the motion, the motion would be denied due to lack of merit based on the defendant's representation that it is not seeking any information of individual customers.
Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, not reported.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-30-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT ZURICH AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Philip L. Pillsbury, Jr., Esq. & Vedica Puri, Esq. of Pillsbury & Levinson LLP 433-8000 for pltf & opposing party; Michael R. Davisson, Esq. of Sedgwick, Detert, Moran & Arnold LLP 213/615-8016 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS "LEGALLY LIABLE" FOR THE FUNDS IN THE POOLED ACCOUNTS. (SHUMM DECLARATION PARAGRAPH 16, EX 1-2004 AGREEMENT, PP 25, 27; AMFs 3-6, 9-12, 14-15, 18.) TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED THAT DEFENDANT DELIBERATELY IGNORED THE FACTS PRESENTED BY THE INSURED WHICH DEMONSTRATED COVERAGE, CHANNELED ITS INVESTIGATION TO TRY TO SHOW THAT AN EXCLUSION APPLIED, MISREPRESENTED THE COVERAGE TO THE INSURED, AND REFUSED TO CONTACT OTHER PARTIES, INCLUDING WELLS FARGO, TO ASCERTAIN WHETHER THE PLAINTIFF HELD AND WAS LEGALLY LIABLE FOR THE FUNDS. (AMFs 21-28.) (In addition, the Court ruled on defendant's objections to evidence, sustained 1, 4, 5. Court stated not adopting any legal conclusions in the declaration/evidence.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses From Deft Zurich American Insurance Company To 1) First Amended Request For Production Of Documents And 2) First Set Of Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Aug-18-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF IRA SERVICES INC'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES FROM DEFENDANT ZURICH AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY TO 1) FIRST AMENDED REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND 2) FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES.
Appearances: Vedica Puri of Pillsbury Levinson (433.8000) for plaintiff and moving party; Mark A. Graf of Sedgwick Detert Moran & Arnold LLP (213.615.8025) for defendant and responding party.
Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported
----
After hearing the court ruled as follows: Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to first amended request for production of documents and first set of special interrogatories is granted. The information sought by plaintiff relating to the loss reserves is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the issue of whether defendant conducted reasonable investigation, was following its own internal procedures for proper investigation and was acting in good or bad faith in denying plaintiff's claim. The facts presented here are similar to those in Lipton which the court finds controlling. Parties notified of the court's ruling by telephone. Plaintiff is directed to submit a proposed form of order.
